---

## KEY FRAMEWORKS BY CATEGORY

### Diagnostic Frameworks

- **5 Levels of Market Awareness** (Schwartz): Unaware → Problem-Aware → Solution-Aware → Product-Aware → Most-Aware
- **5 Levels of Market Sophistication** (Schwartz): Direct claims → Amplified → Mechanism → Amplified mechanism → Identity
- **Message-Market-Media Triangle** (Kennedy): RIGHT message + RIGHT market + RIGHT media

### Sales Copy Structures

- **AIDA** (Classic): Attention → Interest → Desire → Action
- **PAS** (Kennedy): Problem → Agitate → Solve
- **RMBC** (Georgi): Research → Mechanism → Brief → Copy
- **Star-Story-Solution** (Halbert): Character → Journey → Product as answer
- **Six-Step Letter** (Collier): Opening → Description → Motive → Proof → Snapper → Close
- **BAB** (Classic): Before → After → Bridge
- **Four Ps** (Hoke): Picture → Promise → Prove → Push

### VSL & Webinar Frameworks

- **7-Section VSL** (Georgi): Lead → Problem → Mechanism → Proof → Product → Stack → Close
- **Perfect Webinar** (Brunson): Big Promise → 3 Secrets → Stack → Close
- **Epiphany Bridge** (Brunson): Backstory → Desire → Wall → Epiphany → Plan → Transformation

### Email Frameworks

- **Soap Opera Sequence** (Chaperon/Brunson): 5-email serialized story
- **4-Day Cash Machine** (Kern): Value → Value → Offer → Urgency
- **Daily Email** (Settle): Personality-driven daily value

### Offer & Positioning

- **Value Ladder** (Brunson): Free → Frontend → Middle → Backend
- **Hook-Story-Offer** (Brunson): Pattern interrupt + narrative + stack
- **Big Domino** (Brunson): One core belief to knock over
- **Dream 100** (Brunson): Target 100 people who have your audience
- **Attractive Character** (Brunson): Backstory + flaws + polarity

### Psychological Principles

- **Slippery Slide** (Sugarman): Every element's job = get next element read
- **Seeds of Curiosity** (Sugarman): Open loops driving reading
- **30 Psychological Triggers** (Sugarman): Involvement, honesty, authority, belonging, etc.
- **Concept Selling** (Sugarman): Sell the concept, not the product
- **Results In Advance** (Kern): Give genuine result before asking for payment
- **Intent-Based Branding** (Kern): Target based on behavior, not demographics

### Copy Principles

- **Starving Crowd** (Halbert): Market > copy quality
- **Conversation Principle** (Collier): Enter conversation already in prospect's mind
- **Mental Movie** (Collier): Copy creates vivid imagery in reader's imagination
- **Big Idea** (Ogilvy): 5-question test for campaign-unifying ideas
- **Two-Part Unique Mechanism** (Georgi): Root cause + unique solution
- **Research-Driven Creative** (Ogilvy): Weeks of research before writing
- **Copy Assembly** (Schwartz): Copy is assembled from desires + capabilities + message

---

## HEURISTICS (70+ Rules)

### Universal Rules

- Headline = 80% of ad spend (Ogilvy)
- 80% research, 20% writing (Georgi)
- Market selection > copy quality (Halbert)
- Enter conversation already in prospect's mind (Collier)
- Every element gets reader to read first sentence (Sugarman)
- Mechanism separates good from great (Schwartz/Georgi)
- Never go 3+ paragraphs without proof
- Specificity beats vague ($14,537 > "a lot")
- Emotion first, logic second (Collier)
- Fear of loss > hope of gain
- Short sentences get read, long get skipped
- Sell concept, not product (Sugarman)
- Curiosity = most powerful motivator (Sugarman)
- Admitting flaw builds trust faster than any claim (Sugarman)

### Headline Rules

- 5x more people read headline than body (Ogilvy)
- Match headline to awareness level (Schwartz)
- Write 20+ alternatives (Ogilvy)
- NEWS in headline (Ogilvy)
- Short first sentence (Sugarman)

### Email Rules

- One email, one idea
- Under 50 char subject lines
- Follow-up can 2-3x sales (Kennedy)
- Soap opera = can't read out of order (Chaperon)

### Offer Rules

- Build value until "no" feels insane (Brunson)
- 3-5 bonuses each solving specific problem
- Anchor high, reveal actual as fraction
- Urgency must be real
- Always give reason WHY (Halbert/Collier)

---

## METHODOLOGIES (16 Major Systems)

1. **RMBC Method** (Georgi) - Research 40% → Mechanism 20% → Brief 20% → Copy 20%
2. **Perfect Webinar Script** (Brunson) - 60-90 min selling format
3. **Epiphany Bridge** (Brunson) - Story format for aha moments
4. **Six-Step Letter** (Collier) - Empathy-first sales structure
5. **33:33 Timer System** (Schwartz) - Focused writing sessions
6. **Star-Story-Solution** (Halbert) - Narrative selling
7. **Funnel Hacking** (Brunson) - Reverse-engineer what works
8. **20+ Headline Method** (Ogilvy) - Generate and score alternatives
9. **Intent-Based Branding** (Kern) - Behavioral targeting
10. **Mass Control Launch** (Kern) - 4-phase launch sequence
11. **Results In Advance** (Kern) - Value-first strategy
12. **Email Sequence Types** - Soap opera, daily, nurture, launch, welcome, cart abandon
13. **Value Ladder Build** (Brunson) - Free → frontend → middle → backend
14. **A-Pile Strategy** (Halbert) - Personal mail formatting
15. **Slippery Slide Optimization** (Sugarman) - Flow engineering
16. **Conversation Entry** (Collier) - Meet prospect where they are

| Author | Key Terms |
|--------|-----------|
| Schwartz | awareness, sophistication, mechanism, fascinations, mass desire |
| Halbert | starving crowd, A-pile, swipe file, motion, P.S. |
| Ogilvy | Big Idea, research, brand image, positioning |
| Kennedy | no B.S., message-market-media, magnetic, direct response |
| Georgi | RMBC, mechanism, root cause, brief, systematic |
| Brunson | funnel, value ladder, stack, epiphany, hook-story-offer |
| Sugarman | slippery slide, triggers, seeds, curiosity, concept |
| Collier | conversation, mental movie, imagine, vivid |
| Kern | intent, behavioral, results in advance, authentic |

## QUICK REFERENCE: WHICH FRAMEWORK FOR WHICH TASK

| Task | Primary Framework |
|------|-------------------|
| Headline writing | Schwartz Awareness + Ogilvy 20+ method |
| Sales letter | Halbert SSS + Collier 6-step |
| VSL script | Georgi RMBC + 7-section |
| Webinar | Brunson Perfect Webinar |
| Email sequence | Chaperon Soap Opera + Kern 4-Day |
| Offer creation | Brunson Value Stack + Hook-Story-Offer |
| Market diagnosis | Schwartz 5 Levels |
| Campaign strategy | Kennedy Message-Market-Media |
| Copy flow | Sugarman Slippery Slide |
| Copy research | Georgi RMBC Phase R + Ogilvy research |

## SOURCE QUALITY NOTE

SECONDARY source (curated by Synkra AIOS). Weight: 0.70. Frameworks are well-structured but not from direct transcriptions. Useful as reference and gap-filling for existing DNAs (especially Russell Brunson).
